name: Oscar Brown, Jr.
description: American recording artist; singer, songwriter, playwright, poet, civil rights activist, and actor (1926–2005)
abstract: Oscar Brown Jr. (October 10, 1926 – May 29, 2005) was an American singer, songwriter, playwright, poet, actor, and civil rights activist (Brown was African-American).

works_created.t: Living Double in a World of Trouble

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: We mentioned Oscar Brown a few minutes ago. I think it was ’64. You guys did a show at the Waldorf Astoria in New York. [INTERVIEWEE] We did?

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar Brown and Maya are the ones who encouraged me to see myself as a writer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Oscar Peterson, Oscar Brown, and Ray Brown trio
